filled.diagnosis_and_prognosis: The claimant sustained soft tissue injuries to the head and left lower leg as a result of the index accident. He also sustained three lacerations to the scalp which have now well-healed. The head pain resolved within approximately four days. The left lower leg pain, which was initially severe to moderate, has now reduced to mild and is weather-sensitive. All physical injuries have resolved or are resolving satisfactorily and no further physiotherapy or investigation is required. The claimant continues to experience psychological symptoms including nightmares, flashbacks, heightened startle response, and depression, for which a referral to a psychologist is recommended.
filled.effect_on_life: As a consequence of the index accident, the claimant was unable to ride his electric bicycle for approximately two months and was required to use a taxi to travel to work for approximately two and a half months. He reports no significant impact upon leisure activities, holidays, or family plans. There were no changes to his living arrangements. The claimant reports that he was too frightened to cycle for a prolonged period and remains startled by car horns.
filled.effect_on_work: The claimant was absent from work for one week following the index accident. Upon his return, he was placed on lighter duties for approximately six weeks before resuming his normal role. He works twelve-hour shifts as a factory operative and reports that on occasions during the recovery period he was required to leave work after eight hours owing to pain in his leg and back.
examination_findings: Three well-healed lacerations were noted on the top of the scalp, reported to be secondary to the index accident.
No tenderness was identified on palpation of the left lower leg or knee.
Screening examination of the left hip was normal with no pain on movement.
The left knee was ligamentously stable.
Range of motion at the left hip, knee, and ankle was normal.
No areas of bony tenderness were identified in the left lower limb.
No current head pain was reported at the time of examination; pins and needles sensation on the scalp was noted but attributed to a pre-existing condition.
filled.immediate_medical_attendance: The claimant was attended to at the scene by both the police and ambulance services. He was transported by ambulance to Pinderfields Hospital Accident and Emergency department, where he underwent an X-ray and was examined. He was advised to rest and take paracetamol. He was discharged after approximately seven to eight hours.
filled.incident_description: The claimant was riding his electric bicycle on Huddersfield Road at approximately 05:40 to 05:50 hours in the morning, returning home following a night shift. A motor vehicle emerged from a side road into the main road without giving way. The claimant, who had the right of way, was unable to avoid the vehicle and collided with it in a T-bone fashion. Upon impact, the claimant struck the windscreen of the vehicle with his head, rolled over the vehicle approximately twice, and fell to the ground on the other side of the car. The driver of the vehicle stopped and called the emergency services.
filled.living_arrangements: No change to living arrangements following the accident.
filled.medical_history: The claimant has a history of a previous cerebrovascular accident (stroke), hypertension, and chronic kidney disease. He reports taking regular medication. He also reports pre-existing pins and needles sensation on the top of his head, which predates the index accident.
physical_symptoms: Head pain described as mild to moderate on the day of the accident, which resolved within approximately four days.
Three lacerations to the scalp sustained in the accident, now well-healed.
Left lower leg pain described as severe to moderate on the day of the accident, now reported as mild.
Extensive bruising to the back and legs noted the day following the accident.
Generalised body aching the day following the accident.
Weather-sensitive aching in the left knee, which the claimant reports was not present prior to the accident.
filled.pre_existing_conditions: The claimant has a history of a previous stroke, hypertension, and chronic kidney disease. He also reports a pre-existing pins and needles sensation on the top of his head, which predates the index accident.
filled.psychological_symptoms: The claimant reports experiencing nightmares following the accident, in which he relives the collision and wakes to the sensation of the impact. These nightmares persist to the present day. He was too frightened to ride his bicycle for approximately two months following the accident and remains startled by car horns. He also reports symptoms of depression, which he states are worsening, compounded by the subsequent death of his wife. A referral to a psychologist is recommended.
filled.rehabilitative_treatment: The claimant attended approximately four sessions of physiotherapy at a facility in Huddersfield, commencing in August 2025, which was arranged by the insurance company. The sessions focused on exercises for his left leg. The claimant discontinued physiotherapy owing to his wife's diagnosis of cancer, which required him to act as her full-time carer. A referral to a psychologist has been recommended for the claimant's ongoing psychological symptoms.
filled.subsequent_medical_attendance: The claimant did not attend his general practitioner following discharge from hospital. He self-managed his symptoms with over-the-counter analgesics. He commenced physiotherapy in approximately August 2025, arranged by the insurance company, attending approximately four sessions before discontinuing due to his wife's illness.
